definition: having more than one possible meaning; unclear
exampleSentence: The ambiguous instructions left the students confused about what to do.

definition: pleasant and friendly; agreeable in nature
exampleSentence: The congenial host made every guest feel right at home.

definition: lasting for a very short time
exampleSentence: The beauty of the cherry blossoms is ephemeral, lasting only a few days.

definition: having or displaying a passionate intensity
exampleSentence: The fervent crowd cheered passionately for their favorite singer.

definition: to move or act quickly; to speed up
exampleSentence: She hastened to finish her homework before dinner.

definition: to place two things close together to compare or contrast them
exampleSentence: The artist chose to juxtapose light and dark colors in the painting.

definition: very generous or forgiving, especially toward a rival or enemy
exampleSentence: The magnanimous winner congratulated his opponent and praised his effort.

definition: feeling or appearing casually calm and relaxed; not worried
exampleSentence: She tried to act nonchalant even though she was nervous about the exam.

definition: to make someone less angry or upset; to calm down
exampleSentence: The manager offered a refund to placate the unhappy customer.

definition: complaining in a whiny or petulant way
exampleSentence: The querulous child whined about everything during the long car ride.

definition: briefly and clearly expressed; using few words
exampleSentence: The teacher asked for a succinct answer, not a long essay.

definition: a feeling of fear or anxiety about something that may happen
exampleSentence: She approached the stage with trepidation before her first speech.

definition: having no moral principles; dishonest and unfair
exampleSentence: The unscrupulous salesman tricked elderly customers into buying things they did not need.

definition: to regard with great respect and admiration
exampleSentence: Many cultures venerate their elders for their wisdom and experience.

definition: sudden and unexpected; brief to the point of being rude
exampleSentence: The abrupt ending of the movie left the audience wanting more.

definition: correct in all details; exact and without errors
exampleSentence: The weather forecast turned out to be surprisingly accurate.

definition: to make changes to improve or correct something
exampleSentence: The senator proposed to amend the bill before the final vote.

definition: a short, amusing or interesting story about a real event or person
exampleSentence: The speaker shared a funny anecdote about her childhood.

definition: a lack of interest, enthusiasm, or concern
exampleSentence: The students showed apathy toward the school election and few bothered to vote.

definition: strict and serious in manner; having no comforts or luxuries
exampleSentence: The austere classroom had only desks, chairs, and a chalkboard.

definition: a light or signal used to guide or warn; a source of inspiration
exampleSentence: The lighthouse served as a beacon for ships navigating the rocky coast.

definition: gentle and kind; not harmful
exampleSentence: The doctor assured the patient that the tumor was benign and not dangerous.

definition: large in quantity; generous and giving freely
exampleSentence: The bountiful harvest provided enough food for the entire village.

definition: shortness of time or length; being brief
exampleSentence: The brevity of his speech surprised everyone who expected a long talk.

definition: a sudden event that causes great damage or suffering
exampleSentence: The earthquake was a terrible calamity that destroyed hundreds of homes.

definition: showing a cruel lack of concern for others' feelings
exampleSentence: His callous remarks about the accident hurt everyone who heard them.

definition: to attract and hold the attention or interest of someone
exampleSentence: The magician's performance captivated the entire audience.

definition: careful to avoid potential problems or dangers
exampleSentence: The cautious driver slowed down when the road became icy.

definition: to climb or move in an awkward and difficult way using hands and feet
exampleSentence: The children clambered over the rocks to reach the tide pools.

definition: the quality of being clear and easy to understand
exampleSentence: The teacher praised the clarity of her essay and its well-organized ideas.

definition: to work together with others on a project or task
exampleSentence: The two scientists decided to collaborate on their research project.

definition: feeling so satisfied with oneself that one does not try harder
exampleSentence: The team became complacent after winning several games in a row.

definition: the state of being calm and in control of one's feelings
exampleSentence: She maintained her composure even when the audience asked tough questions.

definition: to take someone's property by authority
exampleSentence: The teacher had to confiscate the student's phone during the test.

definition: to face up to and deal with a difficult situation or person
exampleSentence: She decided to confront the bully instead of staying silent.

definition: permission for something to happen or agreement to do something
exampleSentence: You must get your parents' consent before going on the field trip.

definition: to secretly plan with others to do something harmful or illegal
exampleSentence: The characters in the novel conspire to overthrow the unjust king.

definition: causing or likely to cause arguments and disagreement
exampleSentence: The contentious debate lasted for hours with neither side backing down.

definition: warm and friendly
exampleSentence: The new neighbors gave us a cordial welcome when we moved in.

definition: to slowly destroy or damage metal or other material through a chemical reaction
exampleSentence: Saltwater can corrode the metal parts of a boat if not properly maintained.

definition: to shrink back in fear, embarrassment, or discomfort
exampleSentence: He could not help but cringe when he heard his own voice on the recording.

definition: large, heavy, and difficult to carry or use
exampleSentence: The cumbersome suitcase was too heavy for her to lift onto the shelf.

definition: to reduce or limit something
exampleSentence: The school decided to curtail recess time due to the cold weather.

definition: seeming difficult to deal with; intimidating
exampleSentence: Writing a twenty-page research paper was a daunting task for the students.

definition: skillful and quick in one's movements or actions
exampleSentence: The deft surgeon completed the operation in record time.

definition: outward behavior or manner toward others
exampleSentence: Her calm demeanor helped the nervous students relax before the exam.

definition: to publicly declare that something is wrong or evil
exampleSentence: Several leaders denounced the act of violence in strong terms.

definition: empty and lacking people or life; feeling very lonely and sad
exampleSentence: The desolate landscape of the abandoned town was eerie and quiet.

definition: to depart from an established course or standard
exampleSentence: The pilot had to deviate from the flight plan due to bad weather.

definition: to plan or invent a method or idea through careful thought
exampleSentence: The students worked together to devise a plan for their science project.

definition: to make something seem worthy or important
exampleSentence: She refused to dignify the rude question with a response.

definition: disagreement or lack of harmony between people
exampleSentence: There was discord among the team members about the best strategy.

definition: careful and cautious to avoid attracting attention or causing offense
exampleSentence: She was discreet about the surprise party so no one found out.

definition: to gradually become smaller or less
exampleSentence: Their food supplies began to dwindle as the journey continued.

definition: showing sincere and intense conviction; serious
exampleSentence: He made an earnest promise to help his friend through the difficult time.

definition: to draw out a response or reaction from someone
exampleSentence: The comedian's jokes were able to elicit laughter from the entire crowd.

definition: a serious effort or attempt to do something
exampleSentence: Learning a new language is a challenging but rewarding endeavor.

definition: unpredictable and inconsistent in behavior or movement
exampleSentence: The erratic weather changed from sunny to stormy within minutes.

definition: respect and admiration for someone
exampleSentence: The beloved teacher was held in high esteem by all her students.

definition: to apply force, effort, or influence
exampleSentence: She had to exert all her strength to push open the heavy gate.

definition: to make someone feel very happy, excited, or thrilled
exampleSentence: The roller coaster ride exhilarated everyone who tried it.

definition: possible and practical to do or achieve
exampleSentence: The committee discussed whether the new park project was financially feasible.

definition: extremely fierce or violent
exampleSentence: The ferocious storm knocked down trees and power lines across the city.

definition: to rise and fall irregularly in number or amount
exampleSentence: Gas prices tend to fluctuate throughout the year.

definition: pitifully sad, lonely, or abandoned
exampleSentence: The forlorn puppy sat at the shelter door waiting for someone to adopt it.

definition: inspiring fear or respect through being impressively large, powerful, or capable
exampleSentence: The chess champion proved to be a formidable opponent.

definition: not having any serious purpose or value
exampleSentence: She avoided frivolous spending and saved money for important things.

definition: a person's manner of walking
exampleSentence: The horse had a smooth, steady gait that made riding comfortable.

definition: extremely thin and bony, especially because of illness or hunger
exampleSentence: After weeks lost in the wilderness, the hikers looked gaunt and exhausted.

definition: to stare in an angry or fierce way
exampleSentence: The teacher gave the noisy students a stern glare to quiet them down.

definition: to give someone pleasure or satisfaction
exampleSentence: It gratified her to see her hard work finally pay off.

definition: clever but sometimes dishonest behavior used to trick people
exampleSentence: The fox used guile to escape the trap set by the hunters.

definition: to prevent the free movement or progress of something
exampleSentence: Heavy rain hampered the construction crew's progress on the new building.

definition: able to endure difficult conditions; strong and robust
exampleSentence: Only hardy plants can survive the freezing temperatures of the tundra.

definition: widespread destruction or chaos
exampleSentence: The tornado wreaked havoc on the small town, damaging dozens of homes.

definition: behaving in a superior or arrogant way toward others
exampleSentence: Her haughty attitude made it hard for her to make friends.

definition: a place of safety or refuge
exampleSentence: The library was a quiet haven where she could study in peace.

definition: to be a sign that something important is about to happen; to announce
exampleSentence: The first warm breeze of spring heralded the end of winter.

definition: extremely happy, peaceful, or picturesque
exampleSentence: They spent an idyllic afternoon reading by the lake.

definition: in a fair way that treats all sides equally
exampleSentence: The referee judged the competition impartially without showing any favoritism.

definition: acting quickly without thought or care
exampleSentence: His impetuous decision to quit his job surprised everyone.

definition: continuing without stopping; nonstop
exampleSentence: The incessant barking of the neighbor's dog kept everyone awake.

definition: feeling or showing anger because of something that is unfair
exampleSentence: She was indignant when she was falsely accused of cheating.

definition: to figure out something based on evidence rather than direct statements
exampleSentence: From the clues in the story, the reader can infer that the character is hiding something.

definition: to cause something unpleasant to be suffered by someone
exampleSentence: The bully tried to inflict pain on others to feel powerful.

definition: to cause an event or situation to happen by one's actions
exampleSentence: The troublemaker tried to instigate a fight between the two groups.

definition: fearless and adventurous
exampleSentence: The intrepid explorer ventured deep into the uncharted jungle.

definition: to irritate or annoy someone
exampleSentence: The constant tapping of his pencil began to irk his classmates.

definition: a joke or witty remark
exampleSentence: He said it in jest, but she took his comment seriously.

definition: to put something at risk of being lost, harmed, or destroyed
exampleSentence: Skipping practice could jeopardize your spot on the team.

definition: of great height; proud and superior in attitude
exampleSentence: The lofty mountains were visible from many miles away.

definition: to feel intense dislike or hatred for something
exampleSentence: She loathes waking up early on cold winter mornings.

definition: giving off light; bright or shining
exampleSentence: The luminous stars filled the sky on the clear summer night.

definition: growing thickly and healthily; rich and luxurious
exampleSentence: The lush green garden was filled with colorful flowers.

definition: to damage or spoil the appearance or quality of something
exampleSentence: A single scratch can mar the surface of an otherwise perfect table.

definition: to wander at random or follow a winding course
exampleSentence: The river meanders through the valley before reaching the sea.

definition: a person or thing that is likely to cause harm; a threat
exampleSentence: Reckless drivers are a menace to everyone on the road.

definition: to repair something that is broken or damaged
exampleSentence: She used a needle and thread to mend the hole in her jacket.

definition: showing compassion or forgiveness toward someone
exampleSentence: The merciful judge gave the young offender a second chance.

definition: to move around and talk to other people at a social event
exampleSentence: Guests were encouraged to mingle and get to know each other at the party.

definition: dull and tedious because of a lack of variety
exampleSentence: The monotonous hum of the engine made the passengers sleepy.

definition: to think about something carefully and for a long time
exampleSentence: She sat by the window and mused about what the future might hold.

definition: to cancel out or make something ineffective
exampleSentence: Eating junk food every day could negate the benefits of exercise.

definition: living a life of moving from place to place rather than settling permanently
exampleSentence: The nomadic tribes traveled across the desert following their herds.

definition: the state of being unknown or unimportant
exampleSentence: The author lived in obscurity before her novel became a bestseller.

definition: stubbornly refusing to change one's opinion or course of action
exampleSentence: The obstinate mule refused to move no matter how hard the farmer pulled.

definition: not able to be seen through; not transparent
exampleSentence: The opaque curtains blocked all the sunlight from entering the room.

definition: to make something extremely dry, especially from heat
exampleSentence: The hot sun parched the farmer's fields during the long drought.

definition: strange or unusual; belonging specifically to a person or place
exampleSentence: The house had a peculiar smell that no one could identify.

definition: deeply thoughtful, often with a sad or serious mood
exampleSentence: She sat in pensive silence after reading the letter from her grandmother.

definition: to become aware of something through the senses or mind
exampleSentence: She could perceive the tension in the room as soon as she walked in.

definition: to die, especially in a sudden or violent way
exampleSentence: Without water, the plants will perish in the summer heat.

definition: never ending or changing; occurring repeatedly
exampleSentence: The perpetual motion of the ocean waves was mesmerizing to watch.

definition: a dangerous, difficult, or unfortunate situation
exampleSentence: The documentary highlighted the plight of endangered species.

definition: to steal goods using force, especially during a war or riot
exampleSentence: The pirates planned to plunder the treasure from the merchant ship.

definition: having a composed and graceful manner; ready for action
exampleSentence: The poised dancer moved gracefully across the stage.

definition: to think about something carefully for a long time before deciding
exampleSentence: He sat quietly to ponder the meaning of the poem.

definition: affectedly grand or self-important
exampleSentence: The pompous man bragged about his accomplishments at every opportunity.

definition: not securely held; dependent on chance or uncertain circumstances
exampleSentence: The vase was in a precarious position on the edge of the shelf.

definition: in its original condition; unspoiled and perfectly clean
exampleSentence: The pristine beach had white sand and crystal-clear water.

definition: to announce something publicly or officially
exampleSentence: The mayor proclaimed the day a holiday in honor of the local hero.

definition: a young person with exceptional talent or ability
exampleSentence: The musical prodigy could play the piano brilliantly at age five.

definition: information used to promote a particular point of view, often misleading
exampleSentence: During the war, the government used propaganda to influence public opinion.

definition: to put an end to something, typically by force; to calm or reduce
exampleSentence: The principal spoke calmly to quell the students' fears about the change.

definition: shining brightly; clearly showing joy or health
exampleSentence: The bride looked radiant as she walked down the aisle.

definition: spreading quickly and out of control
exampleSentence: Rumors were rampant throughout the school after the announcement.

definition: to sharply criticize or scold someone for doing something wrong
exampleSentence: The coach rebuked the player for being late to practice.

definition: to happen again or repeatedly
exampleSentence: The same dream seemed to recur every few weeks.

definition: a place that provides shelter or protection from danger
exampleSentence: The hikers found refuge in a cave when the storm suddenly began.

definition: never stopping or giving up; harsh and unyielding
exampleSentence: The relentless rain lasted for three days and caused severe flooding.

definition: a formal expression of disapproval; a scolding
exampleSentence: The student received a reprimand from the teacher for talking in class.

definition: the act of giving up a position, or acceptance of something undesirable but unavoidable
exampleSentence: He accepted his defeat with quiet resignation.

definition: to officially cancel or take back a decision, law, or privilege
exampleSentence: The school threatened to revoke his library privileges if he returned books late again.

definition: to move about freely without a fixed course or destination
exampleSentence: The deer roam freely through the forest during the early morning.

definition: a long and detailed account of connected events; an epic story
exampleSentence: The book tells the saga of a family spanning several generations.

definition: a place of safety, protection, or shelter
exampleSentence: The wildlife sanctuary provides a safe home for injured animals.

definition: to speak about something in a mocking or scornful way
exampleSentence: The critics scoffed at the idea that the underdog team could win.

definition: to cut off or break apart completely
exampleSentence: The storm severed the power lines, leaving the town without electricity.

definition: nothing but; absolute; extremely steep
exampleSentence: The sheer cliff face dropped hundreds of feet to the ocean below.

definition: to gently calm or relieve pain or distress
exampleSentence: The mother sang a lullaby to soothe her crying baby.

definition: to reject with disdain or contempt
exampleSentence: She spurned his offer to help because she wanted to do it herself.

definition: firmly loyal and unwavering in purpose or belief
exampleSentence: She remained steadfast in her commitment to the project despite setbacks.

definition: requiring or using great effort and energy
exampleSentence: The strenuous hike up the mountain left everyone exhausted.

definition: to become less intense, violent, or severe
exampleSentence: The flooding began to subside once the rain finally stopped.

definition: to urgently call someone to come or to gather one's resources
exampleSentence: The king sent a messenger to summon all the knights to the castle.

definition: to take the place of someone or something, especially by force
exampleSentence: Smartphones have supplanted traditional cameras for most people.

definition: to overcome a difficulty or obstacle
exampleSentence: With determination, she was able to surmount every challenge she faced.

definition: likely to be influenced or affected by something
exampleSentence: Young children are more susceptible to colds and flu.

definition: a planned action or strategy for achieving a goal
exampleSentence: The coach introduced a new tactic to help the team win the next game.

definition: to provoke or make fun of someone with insulting remarks
exampleSentence: The crowd tried to taunt the opposing players to distract them.

definition: the physical features of a stretch of land
exampleSentence: The rough terrain made it difficult for the hikers to advance.

definition: severe physical or mental suffering; to cause great suffering
exampleSentence: The mosquitoes continued to torment the campers throughout the night.

definition: allowing light to pass through so that objects behind can be clearly seen; easy to perceive
exampleSentence: The transparent glass walls gave a clear view of the garden outside.

definition: making a loud, confused noise; emotionally turbulent
exampleSentence: The tumultuous crowd erupted in cheers when the winning goal was scored.

definition: strange or mysterious, especially in an unsettling way
exampleSentence: She has an uncanny ability to predict what will happen next.

definition: to commit oneself to and begin a task or project
exampleSentence: The volunteers agreed to undertake the cleanup of the beach.

definition: to take power or a position illegally or by force
exampleSentence: The general attempted to usurp the throne from the rightful king.

definition: showing strong feeling; forceful and passionate
exampleSentence: She made a vehement argument against cutting the school's art program.

definition: to clear someone of blame or suspicion; to prove correct
exampleSentence: The new evidence helped vindicate the man who had been wrongfully accused.

definition: liable to change rapidly and unpredictably, especially for the worse
exampleSentence: The volatile situation in the region made travel unsafe.

definition: wanting or eating a very large amount of food; having a strong desire for something
exampleSentence: The voracious reader finished three books in a single weekend.

definition: extreme anger; furious rage
exampleSentence: The students feared the wrath of their strict teacher.

definition: great energy or enthusiasm in pursuit of a goal or cause
exampleSentence: The young volunteers worked with zeal to prepare for the charity event.
